attachment.snippet:
1	# Todo
2	
3	This file contains near term tasks with a short description
4	and reference links to more details.
5	
6	Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
7	line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
8	pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.
9	
10	## In Progress
11	
12	When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
13	problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
14	by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder".
15	Each rung is prepended with its commit reference — a literal
16	`[[N]]` placeholder until the commit is pushed, then backfilled
17	to a real file-local `[[n]]` ref (same pattern as the chores
18	As-built rungs):
19	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
20	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
21	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
22	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation
23	
24	**Refactor stage: facade owns topology.** Repo resolution
25	becomes facade state, not per-command preludes — the
26	`validate-desc`/`fix-desc` por equalization and the
27	configurable bot-repo dir; see
28	[the stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-facade-owns-topology).
29	Decisions (2026-07-23): `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]` adopts
30	the symmetric `work = "/"` / `bot = "/.bot"` pair replacing
31	`path`/`other-repo` (identical block on both sides); side
32	detection by location via `find_workspace_root` (root → work,
33	`<root>/<bot-dir>` → bot); presence of `bot` is the dual-repo
34	signal; `.bot` is the new-init default only — existing
35	workspaces keep their recorded dir, no migration.
36	   - [[N]] 0.75.0-0 chore: open facade owns topology cycle (current)
37	   - [[N]] 0.75.0-1 refactor: topology por equalization
38	   - [[N]] 0.75.0-2 feat: topology work/bot config schema
39	   - [[N]] 0.75.0-3 refactor: topology bot-dir sweep
40	   - [[N]] 0.75.0 refactor: facade owns topology
41	
42	## Todo
43	
44	 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
45	 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
46	 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
47	 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
48	 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
49	 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
50	 number. Long-tail entries
51	 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
52	 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](/AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
53	 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
54	 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).
55	
56	1. **Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process; end
57	   subprocess spawning.** Version-control operations are
58	   ~30 hand-rolled `run("jj", …)` spawns plus every
59	   mutation, with per-module private wrappers and raw-git
60	   vestiges in init — stderr parsing instead of typed
61	   errors, and jj's single-attempt index-lock acquisition
62	   (the push `bookmark-set` lock race in [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md))
63	   can't be retried where it fails. A multi-ladder program;
64	   the staged plan, design detail, and the eight absorbed
65	   former Todos live in
66	   [refactor-20260716.md](notes/refactor-20260716.md).
67	   - The remaining stages are the individual
68	     "Refactor stage: …" entries below (listed in program
69	     order; the doc owns execution order, per-stage status,
70	     and design — DRY facade shipped at 0.73.0).
71	2. **Refactor stage: de-gitify init.** Replace init's
72	   strip-jj → git-push → re-colocate flow with the verified

75	   After facade owns topology.

79	   see

81	4. **Refactor stage: stateless push.** Retire the push state

84	   After split push.rs.
85	5. **Refactor stage: jj-lib migration.** Facade internals and
86	   mutations move in-process; the index-lock retry becomes
87	   ours; see

89	   After split push.rs and stateless push.

94	   After the jj-lib migration (program order).
95	7. **Refactor stage: trapezoid close-out.** `push --merge
96	   [<base>]` — the native trapezoid close-out; design settled
97	   in the stage notes; see

99	   After stateless push (no state-file growth) and jj-lib.

103	   repos with the one work-repo template, whose live
104	   `.claude/` doubles as the bot-side seed source; retire

106	   for the new layout. First up after the refactor program.
107	   - `--use-template` rule: explicit `CODE,BOT` copies all

input.old_string:
`.vc-config.toml` already records workspace paths — the
  natural home. Schema shape: the symmetric `code = "/"` /
  `bot = "/.bot"` pair, side detection walking up from cwd
  via `find_workspace_root` (was the deferred half of
  `0.41.1-6`'s title; design in
  [chores-08 Operations](chores/chores-08.md#operations)).
  Open: migration of existing workspaces, the ochid prefix
  recorded in immutable history (old `/.claude/<chid>`
  trailers must keep resolving), the harness-controlled
  symlink location, and the harness's own
  `<project>/.claude` settings/skills dir — renaming the
  bot repo splits it from the vendor's dir.
input.new_string:
`.vc-config.toml` already records workspace paths — the
  natural home. Schema shape decided 2026-07-23: the
  symmetric `work = "/"` / `bot = "/.bot"` pair *replaces*
  `path`/`other-repo` (identical `[workspace]` block on both
  sides — nothing to cross-check); side detection by
  location, walking up from cwd via `find_workspace_root`
  (root → work, `<root>/<bot-dir>` → bot; was the deferred
  half of `0.41.1-6`'s title; design in
  [chores-08 Operations](chores/chores-08.md#operations));
  presence of `bot` is the dual-repo signal (replacing
  presence-of-`other-repo`); POR is `work = "/"` alone.
  - Migration decided 2026-07-23: none — the config records
    each workspace's actual bot dir, so existing workspaces
    keep `.claude` (`bot = "/.claude"`) and their
    `/.claude/<chid>` trailers keep resolving verbatim;
    `.bot` is the *new-init default only* — a
    recommendation, not a mandate.
  - Still open: the harness-controlled symlink location,
    and the harness's own `<project>/.claude`
    settings/skills dir — renaming the bot repo splits it
    from the vendor's dir (only bites a workspace that
    *chooses* a non-`.claude` dir).

aiTitle: Continue VC-X1 refactor with trapezoidal commits

input.old_string: # References
input.new_string:
## refactor: facade owns topology

Commits: see [As-built ladder](#as-built-ladder-2)

Repo resolution is a per-command prelude (each command
re-derives "where is the other repo" from
`path`/`other-repo`); it becomes facade state. Third stage of
the refactor program. Decisions at cycle open (2026-07-23):

- `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]` adopts the symmetric
  `work = "/"` / `bot = "/.bot"` pair, *replacing*
  `path`/`other-repo` — the block is identical on both
  sides, so there is nothing to keep mutually consistent;
  side detection comes from location (walk up from cwd via
  `find_workspace_root`: at the root → work side, at
  `<root>/<bot-dir>` → bot side).
- Presence of `bot` is the dual-repo signal; POR is
  `work = "/"` alone. One vocabulary across surfaces:
  `-s work,bot`, config `work`/`bot`, `Side::Work`/`Side::Bot`.
- `.bot` is the new-init default only — a recommendation.
  Existing workspaces keep their recorded dir (vc-x1 stays
  `bot = "/.claude"`), old `/.claude/<chid>` trailers keep
  resolving verbatim; no migration.

### As-built ladder

- [[N]] 0.75.0-0 chore: open facade owns topology cycle
  - version 0.75.0-0; stage picked into `## In Progress`
    with the five-rung ladder; this section opened; stage
    decisions recorded here, in the ladder block, and in the
    stage section of
    [refactor-20260716.md](../refactor-20260716.md#stage-facade-owns-topology)
  - rider: 0.74.0 ladder-ref backfill (`-3` + close-out
    rungs → [[12]],[[13]])
  - rider: `## Done` retirement sweep — the 0.69.1–0.71.0
    batch (eight entries) migrated to done.md; TODO.md
    references re-packed to `[1]..[5]`
  - rider: the DRY facade and hygiene stages' shipped-status
    links verified in the refactor doc (added at 0.74.0)

# References

---

**Title:** `chore: open facade owns topology cycle`

**Body:**

Open the 0.75.0 facade-owns-topology cycle — third stage of
the jj refactor program: repo resolution becomes facade state,
not per-command preludes. The three cycle-open decisions
(2026-07-23) are recorded in TODO.md, the chores section, and
the stage notes:

- `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]` adopts the symmetric
  `work = "/"` / `bot = "/.bot"` pair, replacing
  `path`/`other-repo`
  - identical block on both sides; side detection by
    location via `find_workspace_root`
  - presence of `bot` is the dual-repo signal; POR is
    `work = "/"` alone
- `.bot` is the new-init default only — existing workspaces
  keep their recorded dir, no migration
- Riders: 0.74.0 ladder-ref backfill, `## Done` retirement
  sweep

File-by-file:

- Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock: version 0.75.0-0
- TODO.md
  - stage picked from Todo into `## In Progress`
    (problem statement, decisions, five-rung ladder); Todo
    renumbered after the removal
  - `## Done` retirement sweep: the 0.69.1–0.71.0 batch
    (eight entries) moved to done.md; references re-packed
    to `[1]..[5]`
- notes/done.md: the eight migrated entries appended, refs
  renumbered into its namespace (`[119]`–`[125]`)
- notes/chores/chores-14.md
  - cycle section opened (intro with decisions, As-built
    `-0` rung with the backfill/migration riders)
  - hygiene `-3`/close-out rungs backfilled
    (`[[12]]`/`[[13]]`)
- notes/refactor-20260716.md
  - stage marked in progress at 0.75.0 with its chores link
  - schema-shape bullet rewritten to the decided form;
    migration decided (none); Open list trimmed to the two
    harness-dir items

---

content.content:
#### `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` — dual-only outliers

- **Shared** — title-matching, ochid extraction, and the
  per-commit walk (`src/desc_helpers.rs` exports
  `extract_bare_id`, `find_matching_commit`,
  `validate_ochid`, etc. and these are topology-neutral).
- **Dual-only** — the entry shape. Both call
  `other_repo_from_config(&config)?` directly
  (`validate_desc.rs:133`, `fix_desc.rs:152`), which errors
  on por instead of resolving to a no-op or single-repo
  validation.
- **Por-only** — none.

The bot thinks equalization here is local: convert the
dual-required prelude into a `default_scope`-style
resolution that no-ops `Side::Bot` when absent. The shared
body doesn't need changes.

#### `init` / `clone` — shared body, dual default

- **Shared** — repo-creation primitives (`init_one` /
  `clone_one` per `chores-07.md > init + clone redesign
  (0.41.1)`), GitHub remote provisioning, push retries,
  template seeding mechanism, `--account` / `--repo`
  resolution via `config.rs`.

### Equalization candidates, ranked

Ordered from closest-to-shared (cheap wins) to
furthest (architectural work):

1. **`validate-desc` / `fix-desc`** — local refactor:
   replace the `other_repo_from_config` prelude with a
   scope-aware resolution that no-ops when `Side::Bot`
   is absent. Body unchanged. The bot thinks this is the
   smallest concrete equalization and a good prototype.
2. **`CommonArgs`-family por test coverage** — pure test
   work; surface bugs in scope handling without
   architectural risk. Likely lands a `FixturePor` use
   in `chid`/`desc`/`show`/`list` test modules.
3. **`init` / `clone` defaulting** — make `--por` /
   `--dual` peer flags rather than dual-default + `--por`
   opt-out; thread the chosen topology through the body
   without changing the inner primitives. User-config
   `[default].topology` (the original user proposal)
   becomes a small follow-on once peers exist.
  guidance.
- Severity / category: *Feature gap* (medium) +
  *Defaulting* (the commands silently assume dual).
- Equalization sketch: The bot thinks the natural shape
  is: detect por topology at command entry, and either
  no-op with a clear message ("por has no other repo;
  validate-desc has nothing to check") or refuse with a
  topology-aware error pointing at `--por`-equivalent
  workflows. The ochid concept stays dual-only.

## 8. Tests and fixtures

- Files touched:
  - `src/test_helpers.rs:68-150` (`Fixture` — dual),

1	//! The `fix-desc` subcommand: scan a revision range and repair
2	//! each commit's `ochid:` trailer against the cross-referenced
3	//! repo — fixing wrong prefixes/lengths, adding missing trailers
4	//! (`--add-missing`), or substituting a fallback value.
5	//!
6	//! Dry-run by default; `--no-dry-run` actually rewrites
7	//! descriptions via `jj describe --ignore-immutable`.
8	
9	use std::path::PathBuf;
10	
11	use clap::Args;
12	use jj_lib::object_id::ObjectId;
13	use jj_lib::repo::Repo;
14	use log::{debug, info};
15	
16	use crate::common;
17	use crate::context::Context;
18	use crate::desc_helpers::{

20	    extract_ochid_from_desc, find_matching_commit, fix_ochid_in_description,
21	    ochid_prefix_from_config, resolve_full_change_id, validate_ochid,

23	use crate::subcommand::SubcommandRunner;

26	/// Fix commit descriptions against the other repo.
27	#[derive(Args, Debug)]
28	pub struct FixDescArgs {
29	    /// Revision (with optional .. notation)
30	    #[arg(value_name = "REVISION")]
31	    pub pos_rev: Option<String>,
32	
33	    /// Number of commits (per open side)
34	    #[arg(value_name = "COMMITS")]
35	    pub pos_count: Option<usize>,
36	
37	    /// Revision(s) to scan
38	    #[arg(short, long, default_value = "@")]
39	    pub revision: String,
40	
41	    /// Number of commits to scan
42	    #[arg(short = 'n', long = "commits", value_name = "COMMITS")]
43	    pub limit: Option<usize>,
44	
45	    /// Maximum number of commits to actually fix/change [default: all]
46	    #[arg(short = 'm', long = "max-fixes")]
47	    pub max_fixes: Option<usize>,
48	
49	    /// Path to jj repo
50	    #[arg(short = 'R', long, default_value = ".")]
51	    pub repo: PathBuf,
52	
53	    /// Path to the other repo [default: from .vc-config.toml]
54	    #[arg(long = "other-repo")]
55	    pub other_repo: Option<PathBuf>,
56	
57	    /// Expected changeID length
58	    #[arg(long = "id-len", default_value_t = DEFAULT_ID_LEN)]
59	    pub id_len: usize,
60	
61	    /// New title to replace the first line (optional)
62	    #[arg(long)]
63	    pub title: Option<String>,
64	
65	    /// Fallback ochid value for IDs not found in other repo (e.g. /.claude/lost)
66	    #[arg(long)]
67	    pub fallback: Option<String>,
68	
69	    /// Actually write changes [default: dry-run]
70	    #[arg(long = "no-dry-run")]
71	    pub no_dry_run: bool,
72	
73	    /// Add missing ochid trailers by matching title and timestamp
74	    #[arg(long = "add-missing")]
75	    pub add_missing: bool,
76	}
77	
78	/// Inputs to the fix-desc op, flat, owned, clap-free.
79	///
80	/// Mirrors `FixDescArgs` field-for-field: positional `REVISION` /
81	/// `COMMITS` (`pos_rev` / `pos_count`), `--revision` / `--commits`
82	/// (`revision` / `limit`), `--max-fixes`, `--repo`, `--other-repo`,
83	/// `--id-len`, `--title`, `--fallback`, `--no-dry-run`,
84	/// `--add-missing`.
85	pub struct FixDescParams {
86	    pub pos_rev: Option<String>,
87	    pub pos_count: Option<usize>,
88	    pub revision: String,
89	    pub limit: Option<usize>,
90	    pub max_fixes: Option<usize>,
91	    pub repo: PathBuf,
92	    pub other_repo: Option<PathBuf>,
93	    pub id_len: usize,
94	    pub title: Option<String>,
95	    pub fallback: Option<String>,
96	    pub no_dry_run: bool,
97	    pub add_missing: bool,
98	}
99	
100	impl From<&FixDescArgs> for FixDescParams {
101	    /// Convert clap-derived `FixDescArgs` into the flat
102	    /// `FixDescParams` (total — every field copies straight over).
103	    fn from(a: &FixDescArgs) -> Self {
104	        Self {
105	            pos_rev: a.pos_rev.clone(),
106	            pos_count: a.pos_count,
107	            revision: a.revision.clone(),
108	            limit: a.limit,
109	            max_fixes: a.max_fixes,
110	            repo: a.repo.clone(),
111	            other_repo: a.other_repo.clone(),
112	            id_len: a.id_len,
113	            title: a.title.clone(),
114	            fallback: a.fallback.clone(),
115	            no_dry_run: a.no_dry_run,
116	            add_missing: a.add_missing,
117	        }
118	    }
119	}
120	
121	impl SubcommandRunner for FixDescArgs {
122	    type Params = FixDescParams;
123	
124	    /// Delegate to the existing `From<&FixDescArgs>` impl above
125	    /// (total — never fails).
126	    fn to_params(&self) -> Result<Self::Params, String> {
127	        Ok(FixDescParams::from(self))
128	    }
129	
130	    /// Run the existing `fix_desc` op.
131	    fn run(ctx: &Context, params: &Self::Params) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
132	        fix_desc(ctx, params)
133	    }
134	}

136	/// Run the `fix-desc` subcommand: scan the resolved revision range
137	/// and repair each commit's ochid trailer against the other repo;
138	/// errors if any commit could not be fixed.
139	///
140	/// `ctx` is unused today (fix-desc reads `.vc-config.toml`, not the
141	/// user config, and doesn't touch the `--log` path); it's present
142	/// for the uniform subcommand-layer signature.
143	pub fn fix_desc(_ctx: &Context, params: &FixDescParams) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
144	    debug!("fix-desc: enter");
145	    let (workspace, repo) = common::load_repo(&params.repo)?;
146	
147	    // Resolve other repo: --other-repo flag, or scope-aware

151	    // topology from `default_scope`, not a flag).

158	                return Ok(());

161	    };
162	
163	    let (other_workspace, other_repo) = common::load_repo(&other_repo_path)?;
164	    let other_config = toml_simple::toml_load(&other_repo_path.join(VC_CONFIG_FILE))?;
165	    let other_prefix = ochid_prefix_from_config(&other_config)?;
166	
167	    let spec = common::resolve_spec(

174	
175	    let (ids, _anchor_index) = common::collect_ids(
176	        &workspace,
177	        &repo,
178	        &spec.rev,
179	        spec.desc_count,
180	        spec.anc_count,

182	    if ids.is_empty() {
183	        return Err(format!("no commits found for revision '{}'", params.revision).into());
184	    }
185	
186	    let root_id = repo.store().root_commit_id().clone();

191	    for commit_id in &ids {

194	        }

202	        } else {
203	            first_line

205

1	# Todo
2	
3	This file contains near term tasks with a short description
4	and reference links to more details.
5	
6	Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
7	line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
8	pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.
9	
10	## In Progress
11	
12	When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
13	problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
14	by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder".
15	Each rung is prepended with its commit reference — a literal
16	`[[N]]` placeholder until the commit is pushed, then backfilled
17	to a real file-local `[[n]]` ref (same pattern as the chores
18	As-built rungs):
19	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
20	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
21	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
22	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation
23	
24	**Refactor stage: facade owns topology.** Repo resolution
25	becomes facade state, not per-command preludes — the
26	`validate-desc`/`fix-desc` por equalization and the
27	configurable bot-repo dir; see
28	[the stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-facade-owns-topology).
29	Decisions (2026-07-23): `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]` adopts
30	the symmetric `work = "/"` / `bot = "/.bot"` pair replacing
31	`path`/`other-repo` (identical block on both sides); side
32	detection by location via `find_workspace_root` (root → work,
33	`<root>/<bot-dir>` → bot); presence of `bot` is the dual-repo
34	signal; `.bot` is the new-init default only — existing
35	workspaces keep their recorded dir, no migration.
36	   - [[N]] 0.75.0-0 chore: open facade owns topology cycle (done)
37	   - [[N]] 0.75.0-1 refactor: topology por equalization (done)
38	   - [[N]] 0.75.0-2 feat: topology work/bot config schema
39	   - [[N]] 0.75.0-3 refactor: topology bot-dir sweep
40	   - [[N]] 0.75.0 refactor: facade owns topology
41	
42	## Todo
43	
44	 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
45	 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
46	 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
47	 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
48	 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
49	 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
50	 number. Long-tail entries
51	 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
52	 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](/AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
53	 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
54	 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).
55	
56	1. **Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process; end
57	   subprocess spawning.** Version-control operations are
58	   ~30 hand-rolled `run("jj", …)` spawns plus every
59	   mutation, with per-module private wrappers and raw-git
60	   vestiges in init — stderr parsing instead of typed
61	   errors, and jj's single-attempt index-lock acquisition
62	   (the push `bookmark-set` lock race in [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md))
63	   can't be retried where it fails. A multi-ladder program;
64	   the staged plan, design detail, and the eight absorbed
65	   former Todos live in
66	   [refactor-20260716.md](notes/refactor-20260716.md).
67	   - The remaining stages are the individual
68	     "Refactor stage: …" entries below (listed in program
69	     order; the doc owns execution order, per-stage status,
70	     and design — DRY facade shipped at 0.73.0).
71	2. **Refactor stage: de-gitify init.** Replace init's
72	   strip-jj → git-push → re-colocate flow with the verified
73	   jj-only sequence; see
74	   [the stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-de-gitify-init).
75	   After facade owns topology.
76	3. **Refactor stage: split push.rs.** Extract `push/state.rs`
77	   so the jj-lib migration reviews cleanly — built and parked
78	   on `support-trapezoid-commits` (0.72.0-1), replay or redo;
79	   see
80	   [the stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-split-pushrs).
81	4. **Refactor stage: stateless push.** Retire the push state
82	   file — derive resume from repo reality; see
83	   [the stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-stateless-push).
84	   After split push.rs.
85	5. **Refactor stage: jj-lib migration.** Facade internals and
86	   mutations move in-process; the index-lock retry becomes
87	   ours; see
88	   [the stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-jj-lib-migration).
89	   After split push.rs and stateless push.
90	6. **Refactor stage: push body-intro validation.** Validate
91	   the commit body opens with a non-dash intro line, with a
92	   clear error; see
93	   [the stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-push-body-intro-validation).
94	   After the jj-lib migration (program order).
95	7. **Refactor stage: trapezoid close-out.** `push --merge
96	   [<base>]` — the native trapezoid close-out; design settled
97	   in the stage notes; see
98	   [the stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-trapezoid-close-out).
99	   After stateless push (no state-file growth) and jj-lib.
100	8. **Restructure templates: single template repo + fixed bot
101	   seed manifest.** Replace the separate
102	   `vc-x1-work-repo-template` + `vc-x1-bot-repo-template`
103	   repos with the one work-repo template, whose live
104	   `.claude/` doubles as the bot-side seed source; retire
105	   `vc-x1-bot-repo-template`. `vc-x1 init` / `clone` updates
106	   for the new layout. First up after the refactor program.
107	   - `--use-template` rule: explicit `CODE,BOT` copies all
108	     non-hidden files from BOT (unchanged — the escape
109	     hatch for rich bot seeds); `CODE` alone seeds the bot
110	     side from a fixed manifest — `LICENSE-*`, `README.md`
111	     — taken from `<CODE>/.claude/`. The `<CODE>.claude`
112	     sibling default is dropped.
113	   - The manifest is the safety property: a live `.claude`
114	     has non-hidden session artifacts at top level, and
115	     the known subset is what lets it double as the seed
116	     source without leaking session history into new
117	     projects.
118	   - Manifest members missing in the source are skipped —
119	     a code template with no `.claude/` content yields a
120	     bare-but-valid bot repo (the bot template is
121	     optional; init already generates the true minimum
122	     itself).
123	   - `memory/MEMORY.md` moves from copied to generated:
124	     it is intentionally empty (seeded only because Claude
125	     tends to create it otherwise), so init emits it like
126	     `.vc-config.toml` instead of copying — no "is it
127	     still empty?" invariant left in the template.
128	9. **ochid: bot-repo location qualifier.** An ochid is
129	   workspace-relative (`/.claude/<chid>`) — nothing in a
130	   published commit says *where* the companion bot repo
131	   lives (vc-x1's is `github.com/winksaville/vc-x1.claude`,
132	   discoverable only by convention). Anyone cloning just the
133	   work repo can't resolve bot-side ochids. Design already
134	   sketched in forks-multi-user.md
135	   [Per-user bot repos via URL-shaped ochid](notes/forks-multi-user.md#per-user-bot-repos-via-url-shaped-ochid):
136	   URL-shaped trailers, plus the complementary
137	   `.vc-config.toml` repo-index form; resolver dispatch is
138	   one rule (URL → fetch, else workspace-relative), existing
139	   path-form trailers stay the backward-compatible case.
140	   - Cheap first rung: declare the companion's URL once in
141	     the committed `.vc-config.toml` (no trailer-format
142	     change; any work-repo clone then knows where the bot
143	     repo lives). Rides naturally with the refactor
144	     program's facade-owns-topology stage
145	     (bot-repo-location config).
146	   - Link rot + mirroring mitigations are in the same doc
147	     section.
148	10. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
149	    baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
150	    change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`
151	    version in commit titles and bodies, `## Todo` /
152	    `## Done` entries, and chores headers — all written
153	    while the work is in progress, i.e. before it lands.
154	    But version numbers are subject to change: in a public,
155	    merge-based flow (e.g. Linux), the version a change

content.text:
Ready to commit — `0.75.0-1 refactor: topology por equalization`:

**Code:**
- `validate-desc` / `fix-desc`: the dual-required prelude replaced — a single-repo/POR workspace now no-ops with a clear `info!` message instead of erroring; dual resolves as before; `--other-repo` still overrides (`src/validate_desc.rs:133`, `src/fix_desc.rs:152`)
- New `common::bot_repo_path(root) -> Result<Option<PathBuf>>` — `default_scope` gates, `scope_to_repos` resolves, `Ok(None)` = no bot side. The prototype of the topology-from-config rule the rest of the stage extends (`src/common.rs:736`)
- `desc_helpers::other_repo_from_config` deleted — these preludes were its last two callers
- Tests: 3 `bot_repo_path` units (dual / single-repo / no-config) + a `FixturePor` end-to-end no-op per command — all pass

Validation: fmt ✓, clippy `-D warnings` ✓, 7/7 suites (451 tests total now) ✓, install ✓. Version `0.75.0-1`; ladder marker flipped; chores `-1` rung written.

Small enough that I did it inline rather than delegating.

content.text:
Commit description for `0.75.0-1`:

---

**Title:** `refactor: topology por equalization`

**Body:**

The cheapest equalization from the 0.61.0 por/dual audit and
the prototype of the topology-from-config rule: `validate-desc`
and `fix-desc` no longer require a dual workspace — topology
comes from the workspace config via the new
`common::bot_repo_path`, and a workspace without a bot side
no-ops instead of erroring. `--other-repo` still overrides.

- Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock: version 0.75.0-1
- src/common.rs: new `bot_repo_path(root)` —
  `default_scope` gates, `scope_to_repos` resolves,
  `Ok(None)` signals no bot side
- src/validate_desc.rs: prelude → `bot_repo_path`; no-op
  path with message; `por_workspace_noops` test
- src/fix_desc.rs: same prelude swap, message, and test
- src/desc_helpers.rs: `other_repo_from_config` deleted
  (its last two callers were these preludes)
- src/common/tests.rs: `bot_repo_path` units — dual /
  single-repo / no-config
- TODO.md: `-1` ladder rung marked done
- notes/chores/chores-14.md: As-built `-1` rung

---

On your approval I'll `vc-x1 push`.

attachment.snippet:
1	//! The `sync` subcommand: fetch + classify + rebase / fast-forward
2	//! a workspace's repos against their remotes, then reposition `@`
3	//! onto the synced bookmark.
4	//!
5	//! `--bookmark` names a **work-repo** bookmark only: the bot
6	//! repo is a linear journal on `main` by design, so every
7	//! per-repo step (tracking preflight, classify, act, reposition)
8	//! resolves its bookmark via `repo_bookmark`, which pins the
9	//! bot repo to `main`.
10	//!
11	//! Sync is a single atomic operation — verify-then-act happens
12	//! inside one invocation against one fetch snapshot (a separate
13	//! check-then-apply pair of runs would race the remote). The
14	//! hidden deprecated `--check` flag preserves the old verify-only
15	//! mode for `push`'s preflight until that is rewired in-process.
16	//!
17	//! **Stop-on-error**: a failure leaves state where the failing step
18	//! stopped so the user can inspect it. Each repo's pre-sync op id
19	//! is persisted to `.vc-x1/sync-state.toml` (see `state`); the
20	//! error report points at `vc-x1 revert` to undo explicitly.
21	//!
22	//! `current_op_id` / `op_restore` are `pub(crate)` so `push` (and
23	//! the `revert` subcommand) reuse the same snapshot-and-restore
24	//! primitives.
25	
26	use std::io::IsTerminal;
27	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
28	use std::process::Command;
29	
30	use clap::Args;
31	use log::{LevelFilter, debug, info, warn};
32	
33	use crate::common::{default_scope, find_workspace_root, prompt, run, scope_to_repos};
34	use crate::context::Context;
35	use crate::desc_helpers::VC_CONFIG_FILE;
36	use crate::jj;
37	use crate::options_flags::scope::{Scope, parse_scope};
38	use crate::subcommand::SubcommandRunner;
39	use crate::toml_simple;
40	
41	/// Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes.
42	///
43	/// One atomic operation: fetch, classify, fast-forward / rebase the
44	/// bookmark as needed, then reposition `@` onto it. On any failure
45	/// the starting state of every repo is restored via `jj op restore`.
46	///
47	/// Repo set is resolved from `-R/--repo` + `--scope`:
48	///
49	/// - `-R PATH` — workspace root, or a single repo to sync alone.
50	/// - `--scope=work|bot|work,bot` — keyword role selection,
51	///   resolved via the workspace root's `.vc-config.toml`.
52	/// - Neither — workspace-default scope:
53	///   - dual workspace (`.vc-config.toml` with `other-repo`) → `work,bot`
54	///   - single-repo workspace (`.vc-config.toml`, no `other-repo`) → `work`
55	///   - POR (no `.vc-config.toml`) → cwd
56	#[derive(Args, Debug)]
57	pub struct SyncArgs {
58	    /// Verify only — fetch + classify; error if any repo needs
59	    /// action; no bookmark move, no `@` reposition.
60	    ///
61	    /// Deprecated and hidden: kept solely for `push`'s preflight
62	    /// shell-out until that is rewired in-process (see the
63	    /// `TODO.md` sync follow-up). Note the fetch still
64	    /// auto-fast-forwards a tracked bookmark — this mode was never
65	    /// fully read-only.
66	    #[arg(long, hide = true)]
67	    pub check: bool,
68	
69	    /// Suppress all informational output (exit code signals result)
70	    #[arg(short, long)]
71	    pub quiet: bool,
72	
73	    /// Bookmark to sync in the work repo. The session (bot) repo
74	    /// is a linear journal and always syncs `main`, regardless.
75	    #[arg(long, default_value = "main")]
76	    pub bookmark: String,
77	
78	    /// Remote to sync against
79	    #[arg(long, default_value = "origin")]
80	    pub remote: String,
81	
82	    /// Rebase a non-empty `@` onto the synced bookmark without asking.
83	    ///
84	    /// After a successful sync, `@` is repositioned onto the synced
85	    /// bookmark. When the work repo's `@` carries changes it normally
86	    /// asks before rebasing; `--rebase` answers yes up front for
87	    /// non-interactive use. Ignored for the bot repo (which
88	    /// always `jj new main`).
89	    #[arg(long)]
90	    pub rebase: bool,
91	
92	    /// Workspace root, or a single jj repo to sync on its own.
93	    ///
94	    /// - `-R PATH` alone — sync just the repo at PATH.
95	    /// - `-R PATH -s ROLES` — use PATH as the workspace root and
96	    ///   sync the named side(s).
97	    #[arg(short = 'R', long = "repo", value_name = "PATH", verbatim_doc_comment)]
98	    pub repo: Option<PathBuf>,
99	
100	    /// Which repo(s) of the workspace to sync.
101	    ///
102	    /// `SCOPE=work|bot|work,bot`:
103	    ///
104	    /// - `work` — sync only the work repo.
105	    /// - `bot` — sync only the bot repo (errors if no bot repo
106	    ///   is configured).
107	    /// - `work,bot` — sync both repos.
108	    ///
109	    /// Composes with `-R` as the workspace root. Default depends
110	    /// on workspace state: dual workspace → `work,bot`;
111	    /// single-repo workspace or POR → `work`.
112	    #[arg(
113	        short = 's',
114	        long,
115	        value_name = "SCOPE",
116	        value_parser = parse_scope,
117	        verbatim_doc_comment
118	    )]
119	    pub scope: Option<Scope>,
120	}
121	
122	/// Inputs to the sync op, flat, owned, clap-free.
123	///
124	/// - `quiet`: `-q` / `--quiet` — clamp output to `Warn` for the run.
125	/// - `bookmark`: bookmark to sync in the work repo (default
126	///   `main`); the bot repo always syncs `main`.
127	/// - `remote`: remote to sync against (default `origin`).
128	/// - `check`: hidden deprecated `--check` — verify-only mode
129	///   (fetch + classify + report, error if action needed; no
130	///   bookmark move, no `@` reposition). Absent ⇒ the normal
131	///   atomic sync.
132	/// - `rebase`: `--rebase` — rebase a non-empty `@` onto the synced
133	///   bookmark without prompting (work repo only; see
134	///   `reposition_work`).
135	/// - `repo`: `-R/--repo` path (None ⇒ discover the workspace
136	///   root from cwd).
137	/// - `scope`: `--scope` parsed (None ⇒ resolve via the
138	///   workspace-default scope at run time).
139	pub struct SyncParams {
140	    pub quiet: bool,
141	    pub bookmark: String,
142	    pub remote: String,
143	    pub check: bool,
144	    pub rebase: bool,
145	    pub repo: Option<PathBuf>,
146	    pub scope: Option<Scope>,
147	}
148	
149	impl From<&SyncArgs> for SyncParams {
150	    /// Convert clap-derived `SyncArgs` into the flat `SyncParams`
151	    /// (total — every field copies straight over).
152	    fn from(a: &SyncArgs) -> Self {
153	        Self {
154	            quiet: a.quiet,
155	            bookmark: a.bookmark.clone(),
156	            remote: a.remote.clone(),
157	            check: a.check,
158	            rebase: a.rebase,
159	            repo: a.repo.clone(),
160	            scope: a.scope.clone(),
161	        }
162	    }
163	}
164	
165	impl SubcommandRunner for SyncArgs {
166	    type Params = SyncParams;
167	
168	    /// Delegate to the existing `From<&SyncArgs>` impl above
169	    /// (total — never fails).
170	    fn to_params(&self) -> Result<Self::Params, String> {
171	        Ok(SyncParams::from(self))
172	    }
173	
174	    /// Run the existing `sync` op.

182	/// `-R` and `--scope` compose:
183	///
184	/// - neither — workspace-default scope against the discovered root.
185	/// - `-R PATH` alone — just that repo.
186	/// - `-s ROLES` alone — roles against the discovered workspace root.
187	/// - `-R PATH -s ROLES` — roles against `PATH` as the workspace root.
188	///
189	/// `pub(crate)` — shared by `sync` and `revert`, which must resolve
190	/// the same invocation shape to the same repos.
191	pub(crate) fn resolve_repos(

193	    scope: &Option<Scope>,
194	) -> Result<Vec<PathBuf>, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
195	    match (repo, scope) {
196	        (None, None) => {

python3 - <<'EOF'
new_head = '''# vc-config: Vibe Coding workspace configuration
#
# work and bot are the repos' paths relative to the workspace root,
# used to resolve changeID paths in git trailers (e.g. ochid: /changeID,
# ochid: /.claude/changeID). The [workspace] block is identical in both
# repos; which side a repo is comes from its location, not this file.

[workspace]
work = "/"
bot = "/.claude"
'''
for f in ['.vc-config.toml', '.claude/.vc-config.toml']:
    s = open(f).read()
    # everything from the first "# Optional keys" line stays
    idx = s.index('\n# Optional keys')
    open(f,'w').write(new_head + s[idx:])
EOF

47	/// Banner string emitted as the first line of normal command runs
48	/// and shown at the top of subcommand `--help` output. Built from
49	/// `Cargo.toml`'s name + version at compile time so it stays in
50	/// sync with the bumped version.
51	const BANNER: &str = concat!(env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME"), " ", env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"));
52	
53	/// Top-level about line — name, version, and the project tagline
54	/// on a single line. Used as the top-level `about` so `vc-x1 -h`
55	/// reads as one banner-plus-tagline header instead of two stacked
56	/// lines.
57	const TOP_ABOUT: &str = concat!(
58	    env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME"),
59	    " ",
60	    env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"),
61	    " - jj workspace tooling"
62	);
63	
64	/// Build the clap command tree with `BANNER` set as `before_help`
65	/// on every subcommand (transitively). Top-level skips `before_help`
66	/// because its own `about` already carries the name+version+tagline.
67	/// Walks via `mut_subcommand` so individual subcommand
68	/// `#[command(long_about = ...)]` blocks don't have to repeat the
69	/// banner text.
70	fn cli_with_banner() -> clap::Command {
71	    fn add_to_subs(mut cmd: clap::Command) -> clap::Command {
72	        let names: Vec<String> = cmd
73	            .get_subcommands()
74	            .map(|c| c.get_name().to_string())
75	            .collect();
76	        for name in names {
77	            cmd = cmd.mut_subcommand(name, add_with_banner);
78	        }
79	        cmd
80	    }
81	    fn add_with_banner(mut cmd: clap::Command) -> clap::Command {
82	        cmd = cmd.before_help(BANNER);
83	        let names: Vec<String> = cmd
84	            .get_subcommands()
85	            .map(|c| c.get_name().to_string())
86	            .collect();
87	        for name in names {
88	            cmd = cmd.mut_subcommand(name, add_with_banner);
89	        }
90	        cmd
91	    }
92	    add_to_subs(Cli::command())
93	}
94	
95	#[derive(Parser, Debug)]
96	#[command(about = TOP_ABOUT, max_term_width = 80)]
97	pub struct Cli {
98	    /// Print the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as the first line, then
99	    /// continue. With no subcommand, prints the banner and exits.
100	    ///
101	    /// Replaces clap's auto-version (which would exit after
102	    /// printing): the banner now rides along with normal
103	    /// subcommand execution rather than gating it, so scripts
104	    /// can capture the version *and* the command's output in
105	    /// one invocation.
106	    #[arg(short = 'V', long = "version", global = true, action = clap::ArgAction::SetTrue)]
107	    pub version: bool,
108	
109	    /// Verbose output: -v debug, -vv trace
110	    #[arg(short, long, global = true, action = clap::ArgAction::Count)]
111	    pub verbose: u8,
112	
113	    /// Log file path (captures all levels)
114	    #[arg(long, global = true)]
115	    pub log: Option<std::path::PathBuf>,
116	
117	    #[command(subcommand)]
118	    pub(crate) command: Option<Commands>,
119	}

139	        Output is a set of items — headers, user, assistant, tool,\n\

141	        --no-<item> (last one wins), with --all / --none as bulk bases.\n\
142	        The default set (headers, user, assistant, tool, summary) can\n\
143	        be replaced by [bot-session].items in the user config\n\
144	        (comma-separated list); CLI flags then adjust the resolved\n\
145	        set. Malformed lines (e.g. a live session's truncated last\n\
146	        line) warn to stderr and never fail the run.\n\n\
147	        Alternate views: --fields (field inventory per entry type),\n\

file.content:
stable ochid target. This is why a bot-repo ochid names `@-`
(the last committed change), not `@`.

### .vc-config.toml

Each repo contains a `.vc-config.toml` that identifies its
location within the workspace, so tools resolve these paths
without repeating the workspace path in every trailer:

```toml
# In the work repo (workspace root):
[workspace]
path = "/"

# In .claude (sub-repo):
[workspace]
path = "/.claude"
```

input.new_string:
### .vc-config.toml

Each repo contains a `.vc-config.toml` recording the workspace
layout, so tools resolve ochid paths without repeating the
workspace path in every trailer. The `[workspace]` block is
**identical in both repos** — which side a repo is comes from
its location (the root vs the root's recorded bot dir), not
from this file:

```toml
[workspace]
work = "/"
bot = "/.claude"
```

content.content:
```

### validate-desc

Read-only scan of commit descriptions against the other repo. The other
repo is read from `.vc-config.toml` (`workspace.other-repo`) by default,
or overridden with `--other-repo`. Reports status per commit: `ok`
(valid ochid), `lost` (ochid: lost), `none` (ochid: none), `err`
(issues found), or `miss` (no ochid trailer).

```
# Validate all ancestors of @ (reads other repo from .vc-config.toml)
vc-x1 validate-desc @..

# Validate with explicit other repo
vc-x1 validate-desc --other-repo .claude @..

# Validate specific range
[workspace]
# workspace.path — This repo's path relative to the workspace root
#   (role-specific: "/" for the work repo, "/.claude" for the bot repo)
#   used by: find_workspace_root, sync, push, validate-desc (structural; written by init)
#   default: (required; role-specific — see init)
path = "/"   # example
```

A key with no default (`default.account`, in the user home)
instead renders a commented example value:


**Repo set resolution.** `-R` and `--scope` compose:

1. Neither — workspace-default scope: `code,bot` if
   `[workspace] other-repo` is non-empty, else `code`. POR (no
   `.vc-config.toml`) → `code` resolved to cwd.
2. `-R PATH` alone — sync just the repo at `PATH`.
3. `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` alone — workspace roles, resolved
   via the discovered workspace root's `.vc-config.toml`
   (`code` → root, `bot` → `root.join(other-repo)`).
4. `-R PATH --scope=ROLES` — roles resolved against `PATH` as the
   workspace root.

<PARENT>/
  remote-code.git/     bare git remote for the work repo
  remote-claude.git/   bare git remote for the .claude bot repo
  <NAME>/              work repo (jj colocated, main tracks origin)
    .vc-config.toml    path="/",       other-repo=".claude"
    .gitignore         /.claude /.git /.jj /target /.vc-x1
    .claude/           bot repo (jj colocated, main tracks origin)
      .vc-config.toml  path="/.claude", other-repo=".."
      .gitignore       .git .jj

toolUseResult.stdout:
```

s = s.replace('-s code,bot', '-s work,bot')
s = s.replace('-s code ', '-s work ')
s = s.replace('(`-s code,bot`)', '(`-s work,bot`)')
s = s.replace('`-s` is keyword-only — `code`, `bot`, `code,bot`, `bot,code`.',
              '`-s` is keyword-only — `work`, `bot`, `work,bot`, `bot,work`.')

s = s.replace('--scope=code ', '--scope=work ')
s = s.replace('`--scope=code|bot|code,bot` alone', '`--scope=work|bot|work,bot` alone')
s = s.replace('(`code` → root, `bot` → `root.join(other-repo)`)', '(`work` → root, `bot` → the root-joined `bot` path)')
s = s.replace('`code,bot` if\n   `[workspace] other-repo` is non-empty, else `code`. POR (no\n   `.vc-config.toml`) → `code` resolved to cwd.',

# remote names (0.74.0-1 missed README)

# schema mentions

#   (role-specific: "/" for the work repo, "/.claude" for the bot repo)
#   used by: find_workspace_root, sync, push, validate-desc (structural; written by init)

#   used by: find_workspace_root, side detection (structural; written by init)
#   default: (required — see init)
work = "/"   # example''')
open('README.md','w').write(s)
EOF

content.text: Checking the failing test first, then your question:

input.command: grep -n "cli_config_validate_unknown" -B5 -A30 tests/cli_config.rs | head -50

content.text:
The failing test answers your question empirically: `cli_config_validate_unknown` writes an old-form `path = "/"` fixture and expects exactly 2 unknown keys — the schema flip makes `workspace.path` a third unknown, so `config --validate` **already flags old keys as unrecognized**.

Current state of the world after this rung, per case:

- **Old-form file** (`path`/`other-repo` only): topology sees no `work`/`bot` keys → treated as POR/single-repo. `config --validate` names the old keys as unknown. So: no hard error, but **silent degradation** at runtime — a dual workspace would quietly sync/validate as work-only until someone runs `--validate`.
- **Both old and new keys**: new keys drive everything; old keys are inert and `--validate` flags them as unknown. No ambiguity, just lint noise.

The silent-degradation case is the one I don't like. Options:

1. **Error with hint (Recommended)** — a cheap legacy check at the topology chokepoints (`default_scope`/`find_workspace_root`/`is_bot_dir` all read the same file): config has `workspace.path` but no `workspace.work` → error "legacy [workspace] path/other-repo schema — rewrite to work/bot (see init's generated header)". Old workspaces fail loudly at first touch, message says exactly what to do, no compat parsing kept.
2. **Warn and degrade** — `log::warn!` at the same spot, behavior stays POR-ish. Softer, but a missed warning still means wrong behavior.
3. **Accept silently** — what we have now; `--validate` is the only signal.

"Both keys present" needs no special handling under any option — new keys win, `--validate` flags the strays.

Which way do you want it? (Test fixture gets updated to match the decision either way.)

content.text:
Ready to commit — `0.75.0-2 feat: topology work/bot config schema`, with your legacy-policy decision folded in:

**Schema flip:**
- `[workspace]` is now the symmetric `work = "/"` / `bot = "/.claude"` pair — `path`/`other-repo` gone; init emits one identical dual block (`ConfigRole::{Work,Bot}` → `Dual`); this workspace's two committed configs flipped in the same commit
- Side detection by location: new `common::is_bot_dir` (parent's config names the dir as its `bot`); `find_workspace_root` keys on `work` and steps up from inside the bot repo; `sync::is_bot_repo` and the new `desc_helpers::ochid_prefix_for` (replacing content-based `ochid_prefix_from_config`) ride it
- `default_scope`/`scope_to_repos` read `bot` (presence = dual signal)

**Legacy files — error fast with the solution (your call):**
- Old-form config (`path`/`other-repo`, no `work`) is still *found* by the root walk, then every resolver rejects it via `common::reject_legacy_config` — the error prints the replacement `[workspace]` block verbatim
- Both-keys files pass (new keys drive; `config --validate` flags the strays)

**Riders:** README caught up (stale "historical wrinkle" paragraph, `-s code` examples, `remote-code`/`remote-claude` names missed by the earlier sweeps, layout/schema snippets); AGENTS.md `.vc-config.toml` section rewritten to the symmetric form.

Validation: fmt ✓, clippy `-D warnings` ✓, 7/7 suites (454 tests) ✓, install ✓, local dogfood ✓ (`chid -s work,bot`, `validate-bot`, `validate-desc` all resolve via the new schema). Note: `sync --check` against origin currently fails on a transient GitHub-tunnel 502 — the push will need the network back.

input.command: grep -n "home\|Args\|arg(" src/config_cmd.rs | head -25; sed -n 180,210p src/config_cmd.rs

content.text:
Good catch — and it exposes a real gap: today `config --validate` has no positional at all (`./claude` is a clap "unexpected argument"), and the workspace branch only ever validates the **root's** file via `find_workspace_root`. The bot side's `.vc-config.toml` is never validated — worse after this rung, since from inside `.claude` the root walk now correctly resolves to the root, so there's *no* cwd from which the bot file gets checked.

Thoughts on `bot|work|<path>`:

- **The vocabulary is right.** A positional `TARGET = work|bot|<path>` reuses the exact keyword set the CLI already speaks (`-s work,bot`) — `work` → root's file, `bot` → the config-resolved bot dir's file, `<path>` → that file (or dir, joining `.vc-config.toml`). Your `./claude` attempt would then just work as `vc-x1 config --validate .claude`.
- **Fix the default too.** In a dual workspace, plain `--validate` should check *both* sides' files, not just the root's — that's the gap, independent of the new positional. With the symmetric schema it could also cheaply assert the two `[workspace]` blocks are identical (drift check, the invariant the schema now promises).
- **Interaction with `--home`:** keep `--home user|workspace|all` as the coarse filter (it also drives the print grouping); the positional subdivides the workspace side. `TARGET` with `--home user` would be a rejected combination. Alternative — fold into `--home user|work|bot|all` — is tempting but conflates "which schema home" (print mode) with "which file" (validate mode); the positional keeps them separate.
- **Sizing:** small — one positional, a resolve step reusing `bot_repo_path`, and the both-sides default. Natural fit as a rider on `-3` (topology bot-dir sweep, where `config` learns the resolved bot dir anyway) or a standalone entry.

Want me to add it as a Todo entry (or fold it into the `-3` rung's plan) when we next touch TODO.md?

aiTitle: Continue VC-X1 refactor with trapezoidal commits

content.text:
Agreed — `--home` reads like schema plumbing leaked into the CLI. My thoughts on the reshaped surface:

- **`vc-x1 config [TARGET]`**, TARGET = `work|bot|<path>`, defaulting to both sides in a dual workspace. For the "both" spelling I'd reuse the scope vocabulary — `work,bot` (and `bot,work`) — rather than introduce a new word `both`; one keyword set everywhere (`-s`, `[workspace]` keys, now `config`).
- **`--validate` flips the mode, positional picks the file(s)** — yes: default action prints, `--validate` checks the same selection. That's the classic verb-flag-over-noun-positional shape and it composes cleanly (`config .claude --validate`, `config work`, plain `config`).
- **One reality-check on dropping the user home entirely:** `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml` does exist and is load-bearing today — `[account]`/`[repo.category]`/`[default]` drive init/clone's remote resolution, and `[bot-session]` has a user-level fallback layer. So the *file* stays; the question is only whether the `config` command surfaces it. I'd say: not by default (repo-specific default, as you say), but keep it reachable — either a `user` keyword in the same positional slot, or just `<path>` (`config ~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml --validate` works with no extra vocabulary). The `<path>` form is the zero-cost answer; a `user` keyword can come later if it earns it.
- **What "print" means per target could sharpen too:** today `config` prints the *schema* (settable keys), which doesn't vary by side. With a target it could print the *file* (or the resolved effective config) for that side — arguably more useful, and it makes `config` symmetric with `config --validate` (same file selection, read vs check). That's a bigger semantic step, worth its own decision.

Net: positional `work|bot|work,bot|<path>` (default `work,bot`), print by default / `--validate` to check, user-home file reachable only via explicit path (for now), plus the both-sides + identical-`[workspace]`-block check in validate. `--home` retires.

Want this as a Todo entry with these decisions sketched, to ride after the `-3` rung?
